skill.name: figma-automation
skill.description: Automate Figma file inspection, component discovery, image export, design token extraction, comments, and project browsing through Rube MCP.
skill.summary: Guide Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through common Figma automation workflows using Rube MCP.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: The static command execution and weak cryptography findings are false positives caused by Markdown inline tool names, examples, and Figma terminology in SKILL.md. The real risk is that the skill requires an external Rube MCP endpoint and an active Figma connection, so users may expose Figma file data to a third-party integration.

medium_findings.title: Third-Party MCP Access to Figma Data
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs users to add the external Rube MCP endpoint and connect Figma through that service. This appears intentional and legitimate, but it creates privacy and authorization risk because Figma file metadata, comments, tokens, and rendered assets may pass through the third-party MCP integration.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The endpoint and Figma connection workflow are explicitly documented. No malicious exfiltration is shown, but the data access path depends on a third-party MCP service.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer Command Execution False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick execution locations are Markdown inline code references to MCP tools and parameters. No executable script, shell command, or Ruby code is present in the reviewed SKILL.md content.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is Markdown guidance and the flagged strings are documented Figma MCP tool slugs or parameter names. I found no evidence of actual command execution.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer Weak Cryptography False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography detections align with words such as Design, ids, and file examples rather than cryptographic functions. No hashing, encryption, or credential handling code appears in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious lines describe Figma design files and supported file types. I found no evidence of weak cryptographic algorithms or crypto API use.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer Reconnaissance False Positives
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance detections refer to Figma team, project, and style browsing capabilities. These are expected Figma workflow features, not local system reconnaissance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged content concerns Figma resource identifiers and temporary image URLs. I found no evidence of host, filesystem, network, or environment reconnaissance.

content.user_title: Automate Figma Workflows with Rube MCP
content.value_statement: Figma data often requires manual lookup across files, nodes, comments, and tokens. This skill gives structured workflows for using Rube MCP to inspect, export, and convert design assets.
actual_capabilities: Guides setup for Rube MCP and Figma connection verification.
Maps Figma URLs to file, node, team, and project identifiers.
Retrieves Figma file JSON, node details, components, and component sets.
Renders and downloads selected Figma nodes as images.
Extracts design tokens and prepares them for Tailwind conversion.
Lists comments, versions, team projects, project files, and team styles.
limitations: Requires an active Rube MCP connection and Figma authorization.
Only supports Figma design files, not FigJam boards or Slides.
Large files or broad node requests can exceed response limits.
Team IDs must be copied from Figma URLs when needed.

use_cases.title: Inspect a Design File
use_cases.description: Extract file structure, key nodes, components, and component sets from a Figma design URL.

use_cases.title: Export Production Assets
use_cases.description: Render selected frames or components as images and download them with clear names.

use_cases.title: Convert Design Tokens
use_cases.description: Extract colors, typography, spacing, and variables before converting tokens for Tailwind use.

prompt_templates.title: Check Figma Connection
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the figma-automation skill to verify Rube MCP is available, search for current Figma tools, and confirm my Figma connection status.

prompt_templates.title: Summarize a Figma File
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the figma-automation skill to inspect this Figma design URL. Summarize pages, major frames, components, and any file limitations you find.

prompt_templates.title: Export Selected Assets
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the figma-automation skill to find the node IDs in this Figma URL, render the selected frames as PNG images, and download them with descriptive names.

prompt_templates.title: Extract Design Tokens for Tailwind
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the figma-automation skill to extract local styles and variables from this Figma file, then convert the complete token set for Tailwind configuration.

output_examples.input: A designer shares a Figma design URL and asks for a file overview.
output_examples.output: A concise file summary with pages, important frames, component sets, missing access issues, and suggested next nodes to inspect.

output_examples.input: A developer asks for exportable images from three selected frames.
output_examples.output: A short export report listing rendered frame names, image formats, download results, and any frames that failed to render.

output_examples.input: A design system owner asks for Tailwind-ready tokens.
output_examples.output: A token extraction summary covering colors, typography, spacing, variables, conversion status, and any unsupported token fields.

best_practices: Search Rube MCP tools first so parameter schemas match the current integration.
Use narrow node IDs and shallow depth before requesting larger file sections.
Confirm Figma authorization and user intent before reading comments or exporting assets.
anti_patterns: Do not request entire large files when specific frames or nodes are enough.
Do not pass partial token objects into Tailwind conversion.
Do not assume FigJam boards or Slides work with design file endpoints.

faq.question: Does this skill run Figma locally?
faq.answer: No. It guides an AI assistant to use Figma tools exposed through Rube MCP.

faq.question: What authorization is required?
faq.answer: Users need an active Rube MCP connection and an authorized Figma account.

faq.question: Can it export images?
faq.answer: Yes. It documents rendering file nodes and downloading images in supported formats.

faq.question: Can it process FigJam boards?
faq.answer: No. The skill states that FigJam boards and Slides are not supported by these file endpoints.

faq.question: Can it create Tailwind tokens?
faq.answer: Yes. It can guide extraction of full design tokens and conversion for Tailwind configuration.

faq.question: What is the main security consideration?
faq.answer: Figma data may pass through the external Rube MCP service, so users should review access and permissions.
